chunk_summary: This section discusses feeding modifications for infants with gastroesophageal reflux (GER) and highlights red flag features requiring medical attention.
learning_objectives: Understand feeding modifications for managing gastroesophageal reflux in infants.
Identify red flag features in infants with GER that warrant further evaluation.
Learn about the role of feed thickeners in managing GER symptoms.
Recognize when to refer infants with GER for specialized care.
key_facts: Gastroesophageal reflux (GER) is common in infants, with most cases resolving by 12-18 months of age.
Feed thickeners, such as rice cereal, can reduce regurgitation in infants with GER.
Red flag features include poor weight gain, projectile vomiting, blood in stool, and respiratory symptoms like apnea.
Frequent regurgitation without red flags is often managed conservatively with feeding adjustments.
Persistent GER symptoms beyond 18-24 months may require further investigation for underlying conditions.
reference_quote: Feed thickeners in gastro-oesophageal reflux in infants can reduce regurgitation and improve feeding tolerance. (Source: PubMed Central, PMC6045726)
document_key_topics: Feeding modifications for GER in infants
Red flag symptoms in infant GER
Role of feed thickeners in GER management
